The classification of Rutidochlamys mitchellii is that of a genus within the family Proteaceae. This species was first described by the botanist Ferdinand von Mueller in 1864. Rutidochlamys mitchellii is a member of the order Proteales.

intro: Rutidochlamys mitchellii, also known as Mitchell's Rutidochlamys, is a plant species native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rutidochlamys mitchellii include limonoids, coumarins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional Australian medicine to treat a range of health issues.
